popularStory.headline: Godred Crovan (died 1095)
popularStory.description: Godred Crovan (died 1095), known in Gaelic as Gofraid Crobán, Gofraid Meránach, and Gofraid Méránach, was a Norse-Gaelic ruler of the kingdoms of Dublin and the Isles. Although his precise parentage i …

zh.Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿吉爾易斯萊 易曼德
zh.Workington, Cumberland, England, United: 英國英格蘭波藍德沃爾金頓
zh.County Dublin, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 度布林
zh.lifeSummary: 當Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin於大約1040年出生在英國英格蘭波藍德沃爾金頓時，他的父親Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson21歲，母親Ragnfredsdater22歲。他於大約1079年與Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir在曼島結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他於1079年在曼島被列為norse konge på isle of man og hebridene 。他去世於1095年在蘇格蘭阿吉爾易斯萊 易曼德享年56歲，埋葬在曼島。

zh-hans.Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland: 苏格兰阿吉爾易斯萊 易曼德
zh-hans.Workington, Cumberland, England, United: 英国英格兰波藍德沃爾金頓
zh-hans.County Dublin, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 度布林
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin于大约1040年出生在英国英格兰波藍德沃爾金頓时，他的父亲Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson21岁，母亲Ragnfredsdater22岁。他于大约1079年与Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir在曼岛结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他于1079年在曼岛被列为norse konge på isle of man og hebridene 。他去世于1095年在苏格兰阿吉爾易斯萊 易曼德享年56岁，埋葬在曼岛。

nameMeanings.name: Haraldsson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Haraldsson: patronymic from the personal name Harald, Old Norse Haraldr (see Harold ). This is also an Americanized form of the Norwegian cognate Haraldsen. Compare Haralson , Haroldsen , Haroldson , and Harrelson .

variants.name: Harrelson
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian Haraldsen and, in some cases, also of Swedish Haraldsson (see Haraldson ). Compare Harelson and Harralson .

History: The first known bearer of the surname Harrelson was Paul Harrelson, Sr (in some sources his surname is spelled Harralson), born in the middle of the 17th century in Denmark or Norway (Norway was in a union with Denmark at that time), who in the 1670s or 1680s settled in New Kent County, VA. There appears to be only a few later immigrants from Norway or Sweden, whose Americanized surname was spelled the same way.

variants.name: Harold
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Harold (Old Norse Haraldr, borrowed from an ancient Germanic form Harjawald). Alternatively, the name may arise from the Middle English personal name Harwold, Harold (Old English Hereweald), particularly in Suffolk, where there was also post-medieval confusion with Hurrell . These all go back to an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements heri, hari ‘army’ + wald ‘rule’, which is attested in Europe from an early date; the Roman historian Tacitus records a certain Cariovalda, chief of the ancient Germanic tribe of the Batavi, as early as the 1st century AD See also Herald .
English: habitational name from Harrold (Bedfordshire), from Old English hār ‘gray’ + wald ‘large tract of woodland, high woodland’.

Irish: this name is of direct Norse origin (see 1 above), but is also occasionally a variant of Harrell and Hurrell . Spellings without final d have been adopted as the Anglicized form of the Gaelic Ó hEarghail (see Harrell ).

variants.name: Haralson
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian Haraldsen and Swedish Haraldsson (see Haraldson ).

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Isle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from a Lincolnshire variant of   Iles  . See also   Lisle   and compare   Lill  .

variants.name: Iles
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Lisle   (see   Lyle  ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Man
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Chan, Cheuk, Kin, Kwok, Sai, Shek, Tong, Chi, Choong, Chung Yee, Hin.
Flemish and Dutch (also De Man): from Middle Dutch man ‘man, husband, vassal’ (compare Mann ).
English: variant of   Mann  .

variants.name: Mann
meanings: English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a fierce or strong man, or for a man contrasted with a boy, from Middle English, Middle High German man, German Mann ‘man’. In some cases it may have arisen as an occupational name for a servant, from the medieval use of the term to describe a person of inferior social status. The Jewish surname can be artificial.

English and German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Manno, found in Old English as Mann or Manna. This originated either as a byname or else as a short form of a compound name containing the element man ‘man’, such as Hermann .
English: habitational name from the Isle of Man.

variants.name: Mendel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Florian, Kurt, Alois, Monika, Taube.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Mendl, a diminutive of Man (see Man 9).
South German: variant of   Meindl  . This surname is also found in France (mainly Lorraine and Alsace).

variants.name: Maan
meanings: Indian (Punjab): Jat and Sikh name of unexplained etymology, borne by one of the three oldest Jat tribes to settle in the subcontinent.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 滿, 萬, 文, 閔, and 聞. See   Man   5-8.
Muslim (mainly Iraq and Syria): habitational name from any of several places called Maʿān, in Arabia and the Levant.

variants.name: Manne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Anuradha, Kiran, Kishore, Murthy, Prabhakar, Prasad, Rao.  Jewish Akiva, Shmuel, Yaakov.
Indian (Andhra Pradesh; pronounced as two syllables): unexplained.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal name Mane, a variant of Man 9.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Dublin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Staffordshire): apparently a habitational name referring to Dublin, the capital of Ireland, but if so it would have been adopted outside Ireland, since placenames were not normally used as surnames in Ireland.

variants.name: Doublin
meanings: English: variant of Dublin . The surname Doublin is no longer found in Britain.
History: In North America, the surname Doublin can be traced back to James Doublin/Dublin born in NC in 1780.

nameMeanings.name: Godred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Goodread  .

variants.name: Goodread
meanings: English: (i) relationship name from the Old Scandinavian personal name Guðrøðr, possibly in some cases a nickname from Middle English god rede ‘good counsel’. Compare Scottish Meiklereid and Robert Smalred, 1176 in Pipe Rolls (Yorks).

nameMeanings.name: "Crovan"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a diminutive of crón ‘swarthy’.

variants.name: Cronan
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a diminutive of crón ‘swarthy’.

variants.name: Cronin
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a diminutive of crón ‘swarthy’.

en.lifeSummary: When Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin was born about 1040, in Workington, Cumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson, was 21 and his mother, Ragnfredsdater, was 22. He married Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir about 1079, in Isle of Man. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. In 1079, his occupation is listed as norse konge på isle of man og hebridene  in Isle of Man. He died in 1095, in Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland, at the age of 56, and was buried in Isle of Man.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin est né environ 1040, à Workington, Cumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Ragnfredsdater, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir environ 1079, à Île de Man. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. En 1079, sa profession de son indiquée est norse konge på isle of man og hebridene  à Île de Man. Il est décédé en 1095, à Islay Island, Argyll, Écosse, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Île de Man.

de.lifeSummary: Als Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin um 1040 in Workington, Cumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson 21 und seine Mutter Ragnfredsdater 22. Er hat um 1079 in Isle of Man Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. 1079 ist sein Beruf als norse konge på isle of man og hebridene  in Isle of Man aufgeführt. Er starb 1095 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland und wurde in Isle of Man begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin è nato il circa 1040 nel Workington, Cumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Ragnfredsdater, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir circa 1079, nel Isola di Man. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Nel 1079, la sua occupazione è indicata come norse konge på isle of man og hebridene  nel Isola di Man. Morì nel 1095, nel Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Isola di Man.

ja.Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland: スコットランドアルグイルイスレイ イスランド
ja.Workington, Cumberland, England, United: イギリスイギリスクウンバーランドウオルキントン
ja.County Dublin, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ ダブリン
ja.lifeSummary: Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublinが約1040年にイギリスイギリスクウンバーランドウオルキントンで生まれた時，父Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarsonは21歳，母Ragnfredsdaterは22歳であった。約1079年にマン島でQueen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottirと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。1079年，マン島での職業はnorse konge på isle of man og hebridene と記録されている。彼は1095年にスコットランドアルグイルイスレイ イスランドで56歳で死去し，マン島にて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin(이)가 대략 1040년 영국 잉글랜드 쿠벌랜드 워킨그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Ragnfredsdater(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1079년 맨섬에서 Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 1079년, 그의 직업은 맨섬에서 norse konge på isle of man og hebridene 이었다. 그는 1095년 스컷랜드 아그이 이슬레이 이슬랜드에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 맨섬에 묻혔다.

mn.Workington, Cumberland, England, United: Workington, Cumbraland, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin nasceu aproximadamente 1040, em Workington, Cumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Ragnfredsdater, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir aproximadamente 1079, em Ilha de Man. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Em 1079, sua profissão aparece como norse konge på isle of man og hebridene  em Ilha de Man. Ele faleceu em 1095, em Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Ilha de Man.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin родился около 1040 г. в местности воркингтон, кумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Ragnfredsdater, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir около 1079 г. в местности Остров Мэн. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. В 1079 году он работал по профессии norse konge på isle of man og hebridene  в местности Остров Мэн. Он умер в 1095 г. в местности ислеи исланд, аргилл, Шотландия в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности Остров Мэн.

sm.from 1091 to 1094: mai le 1091 e o'o I le 1094
sm.from 1079 to 1095: mai le 1079 e o'o I le 1095
sm.from 1075 to 1094: mai le 1075 e o'o I le 1094

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Godred "Crovan" Haraldsson  King of the Isle of Man and of Dublin nació aproximadamente 1040, en Workington, Cumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Harald “The Black Svari” Ivarson, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Ragnfredsdater, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Queen Ragnhild Maria Haraldsdottir aproximadamente 1079, en Isla de Man. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. En 1079, su ocupación registrada era norse konge på isle of man og hebridene  en Isla de Man. Murió en 1095, en Islay Island, Argyll, Scotland, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Isla de Man.
